content: Next.js App Router calling FastAPI backend: use Route Handlers (app/api/*/route.ts) not Server Actions because Server Actions only support POST and are not designed for proxying to external backends. Route Handlers support GET+POST, allow full HTTP control, and act as a BFF (Backend for Frontend) layer between React components and FastAPI. Client components fetch from /api/* route handlers; route handlers forward to FastAPI. For mutations (remember, feedback, forget) Server Actions are also viable since they are POST-only — but Route Handlers are more consistent when mixing GET (recall results) and POST (ingest). Recommendation: Route Handlers for all endpoints for consistency.
